title: Lasianthera littoralis

The health benefits of Lasianthera littoralis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its use in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lasianthera littoralis include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lasianthera littoralis include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used to relieve symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Lasianthera littoralis is classified within the family Stilbaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants native to Africa. Stilbaceae is a relatively small family of plants, with around 11 genera. The family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Lasianthera littoralis is native to the coastal regions of South Africa.
